video_title: Top #DDD Bacon, Sausage & Ham Videos with Guy Fieri

restaurants.name: Commonwealth
restaurants.guy_intro: I'm here in Modesto, California, about an hour south of Sacramento. You're going to love this story. Two high school buddies that live in separate parts of the state decide to move back to Modesto because it's a great place to raise their families. And they also decide, hey, why don't we open up a craft beer joint and make scratch-made food? You've got to love the American dream. This is commonwealth.

dishes.description: A plate featuring a bami taco, a barbecue pork taco, and a buffalo chicken taco, all piled on one plate.
A sandwich made with a waffle, filled with cheese, home fries, ham, and eggs, cooked in maple mustard butter.

restaurants.name: The Rally Inn
restaurants.guy_intro: So I'm here in Cleveland. Happens to be a joint that's serving Cleveland comfort food. I got to check it out. This is the Rally Inn.

dishes.description: Mashed Yukon potatoes mixed with shredded hash, wrapped in a whole piece of bacon, fried, and served with a house-made chipotle ranch sauce.
dishes.guy_response: Incredibly creative. You get crispy. You get creamy. You get crunchy, unctuous bacon. ... The chipotle sauce, which is so needed, because it adds a little bit of heat. It adds another little dimension. The creaminess of the ranch cooling it down a touch. Funky, fried, freaky. ... It is delicious and really done right.

restaurants.name: T-Bone Tom's
restaurants.guy_intro: I'm south of Houston, almost by Galveston, in a little town called Kima. ... But there's also this great little eatery here, T-Bone Tom's, where I hear these folks have been hand cutting their steaks for over 35 years.

dishes.description: A 12-ounce ribeye steak marinated for one day in a blend of canola and corn oil, soy sauce, Worcestershire, pineapple juice, granulated garlic, black pepper, and dry mustard, then charbroiled.
dishes.guy_response: You can't identify just one of the flavors. Wow, that's a nice piece of meat. It's the marinade. It's awesome.

dishes.description: House-made Polish sausage, 50% beef and 50% pork, seasoned with seasoned salt, curing salt, and mustard seed, then ground, cased, and smoked for 1.5 hours. Served with house-made barbecue sauce.
dishes.guy_response: Mm. That is about as tender as sausage I've ever tried. ... I like the barbecue sauce. ... Great sausage. ... It's excellent. The barbecue sauce is a really nice complement to it. Mm. Oh, it's delicious.

restaurants.name: Lake Effect Diner
restaurants.guy_intro: So here I am, Buffalo, New York, about five miles northeast of Lake Erie, at a joint that was once a diner, and then changed into a Chinese restaurant, well, then transplanted from Pennsylvania, and then turned back into a diner. And guess what? That's not even the amazing part. These guys are, well, they're curing their own ham, they're corning their own beef, and they're smoking their own bacon. This is Lake Effect Diner.

dishes.description: Boneless pork leg cured for 12-14 days in a brine, then smoked for three hours over mesquite, apple, and hickory woods. Served with red-eye gravy made from smoked ham bones, pan juices, mirepoix, Frank's hot sauce, thyme, and coffee, alongside home fries and eggs.
dishes.guy_response: Mm. This is very flavorful. Tender ham for sure. ... I do think that I would like to have a cup of red-eye gravy in the choc. Mm. Awesome, bud. Nice job.

restaurants.name: Ernest and Son
restaurants.guy_intro: Well, that's unless you're here on Brigantine Island, right over the bridge from Atlantic City, New Jersey. Oh, you got to check it out. This is Ernest and Son.

dishes.description: House-made jerky, not too dry or over-seasoned, with a balance of hot and sweet flavors.
dishes.guy_response: It's not too dry, not over seasoned. It's a nice balance to it. A little hot, a little sweet, right? Yeah. It's good jerky. Great jerky. I'll have another piece.

dishes.description: A sandwich featuring house-made sausage with fennel, peppers, and provolone cheese, served on a fresh roll.
dishes.guy_response: That's good sausage, man. ... That's really good sausage. ... The key is that fennel. You think? Oh, and it gets great flavor all the way through it. ... The bread is nice, tender, but it holds together. Salty provolone. Good, right? Short provolone. You like to eat. I like to eat. Real deal. I love it.

restaurants.name: George and the Dragon
restaurants.guy_intro: So I'm here in southwest Minneapolis on a beautiful, sunny Minnesota day. ... So what do you call a joint that is an English pub with a little Filipino flair? You're right. This is George and the Dragon.

dishes.description: Caramelized onions (with brown sugar and balsamic vinegar) mixed with whipped cream cheese, mayonnaise, Worcestershire, salt, cayenne pepper, dry mustard, and Parmesan cheese, topped with cooked bacon and chives, then baked.
dishes.guy_response: That's ridiculous. You know what? This should be illegal. It's not just onions and bacon. There's much more depth. I think you know what really makes it? It's that mustard. Secret ingredient. A little tang of that, better than good. Excellent. Nice job.

restaurants.name: Be More Pacific
restaurants.guy_intro: I'm here in Houston, Texas. I'm about 10 minutes from downtown in the historic area, here to check out a joint where two buddies who are college roommates opened up a traditional Filipino food truck in Austin. Then they opened a brick-and-mortar in Austin, and then they came down here to Houston to open. Boom, then the pandemic hits. They closed Austin, but Houston's still going strong. This is Be More Pacific.

dishes.dish_name: Longanisa and Spiced Ham Silog Rice Ball
dishes.description: A dish featuring house-made spiced ham and longanisa sausage, served with garlic jasmine rice, topped with a well-done egg, green onion, and homemade banana ketchup.
dishes.guy_response: It's dynamite. The pork loaf is... so tender and super flavorful. ... This sausage, it's got awesome chew, flavor all the way through. Tons of garlic. You better like garlic.

restaurants.name: Fox Brothers Barbecue
restaurants.guy_intro: So, I'm here in Atlanta, Georgia, and I am looking for what? Exactly, some real-deal barbecue. You know what it is, guy? With this Crate 502 in here, we just got to bump the timing up a little bit. That's all it is. So, you call your buddy, Rutledge, who knows a lot about cars and a ton about barbecue. This is the real deal? This is the real deal. This is Fox Brothers Barbecue.

dishes.description: A chopped brisket sandwich made with certified Angus beef brisket, smoked 10-12 hours, served on a toasted bun with house-made jalapeno mayo, a pimento cheese patty, peppered bacon, pickles, tomato, and onion.
dishes.guy_response: Oh, that's a great burger. ... It's super tender brisket. When you chop that brisket up, you get a chance to incorporate that fat. So it makes it even a little bit more unctuous. And adding the pimento cheese and adding the veggies on top of it brings that burger element to it. ... Damn, man. Come on. That's full speed, man. That's all throttle, no bottle, man. That is just getting it done.

restaurants.name: Pizzeria Lola
restaurants.guy_intro: Did you know they have one of the only copper-covered, wood-fired French pizza ovens, a Le Pagnole, at this place, where they're doing off-the-hook pizza? This is Pizzeria Lola.

dishes.description: A pizza with a two-day fermented dough, house red sauce, Wisconsin mozzarella, house-made fennel sausage, and natural casing pepperoni, cooked in a wood-fired oven.
dishes.guy_response: We got this heirloom pork with the real fat in it and the real flavor. And the fennel is rocking this. ... This is way beyond your everyday pizza joint on the corner, sausage and pepperoni. You got to try this one. It's the most heightened, delicious pepperoni sausage pizza you've ever had. You're like, this is what pepperoni is supposed to taste like.
